source: Google Fact Check Tools API (claims:search; ClaimReview markup)
scope: Independent third-party corroboration for the corpus's DEBUNKABLE pop-myth slice ONLY. Published fact-checkers do not review authorship/provenance, so the source-discipline CORE is necessarily uncovered — reported, not hidden.

coreCoverage.note: expected ~0 — fact-checkers do not cover literary attribution

myths.claim: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye

factChecks.claimText: The Great Wall of China is the only man-made object visible from the moon.

myths.claim: Marco Polo introduced pasta to Italy
Medieval Europeans believed the earth was flat
Vikings wore horned helmets in battle
Humans only use 10 percent of their brain

factChecks.claimText: Humans only use 10% of their brains.

myths.claim: People have distinct left-brained or right-brained personalities
Students learn better when taught in their preferred learning style
The tongue has separate zones for different tastes
Hair and fingernails keep growing after death

factChecks.claimText: Human fingernails and hair continue to grow after death.

myths.claim: Christopher Columbus proved the earth was round

factChecks.claimText: The ships used in Columbus’ first voyage across the Atlantic in 1492-93 were called the Niña, the Pinta, and the Santa Maria.

myths.claim: Thomas Edison invented the light bulb

factChecks.claimText: “A Black man invented the light bulb, not a white guy named Edison.”

myths.claim: Bulls are enraged by the color red

factChecks.claimText: Urban legends: Are bulls enraged by the colour red?
The color red triggers bulls, which is why they charge at a matador's red flag (muleta) in a bullfight.

core.claim: Laozi wrote the Dao De Jing
The Yijing was written by a single author

boundary: external corroboration of one slice; not a claim about the core; candidate_only
